findings.evidence: `rg` finds only `normalizedChebyshevCosine_zero` at line 115 and `normalizedChebyshevCosine_one` at line 120, with no `normalizedChebyshevCosine_of_ne_zero`; roadmap lines 331-339 specify `c₀ = π`, `cₙ = π/2` and correspondence with the cosine basis.
findings.fix: Add a positive-degree characteristic lemma, preferably `[simp] lemma normalizedChebyshevCosine_of_ne_zero {n : ℕ} (hn : n ≠ 0) (θ : ℝ) : normalizedChebyshevCosine n θ = Real.cos (n * θ) / Real.sqrt (Real.pi / 2)`.
findings.issue: `normalizedChebyshevCosine` only has `[simp]` normal forms for degrees 0 and 1, but the roadmap target uses `cₙ = π/2` for every positive degree; degrees `n ≥ 2` cannot normalize to the standard cosine basis form without unfolding the definition.

summary: The normalized angular mode API is missing the positive-degree normal form needed to identify the family with the cosine basis uniformly.
